title: Resolve, to Study Stakeholder Input Involving the Appointment of the Commissioner of Marine Resources
last_house_action: 6/4/2025 - Reports READ . On motion of Representative HEPLER of Woolwich, the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report was ACCEPTED . Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
last_senate_action: 6/5/2025 - Reports Read On motion by Senator TEPLER of Sagadahoc the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report ACCEPTED in concurrence. Placed in Legislative Files (DEAD)

actions.description: Received by the Clerk of the House on March 7, 2025. The Resolve was REFERRED to the Committee on MARINE RESOURCES pursuant to Joint Rule 308.2 and ordered printed pursuant to Joint Rule 401.
Carried over, in the same posture, to the next special or regular session of the 132nd Legislature, pursuant to Joint Order SP 519.
Reports READ . On motion of Representative HEPLER of Woolwich, the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report was ACCEPTED . Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
Reports Read On motion by Senator TEPLER of Sagadahoc the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report ACCEPTED in concurrence. Placed in Legislative Files (DEAD)

amendments.label: C "A" (H-462 )
amendments.new_title: Resolve, to Establish a Working Group to Study the Level of Stakeholder Input Involved in the Appointment of the Commissioner of Marine Resources
